title: Is Trump getting the full picture? Vance raises concerns over Iran war
content: US Vice President J D Vance is questioning the Pentagon's assessment of the Iran conflict, concerned about undisclosed missile stockpile depletion. He fears this could impact responses to global flashpoints like Taiwan, South Korea, and Europe.  Despite official assurances, some believe the situation is being downplayed, potentially affecting future military readiness.

title: Riyan Parag captured vaping inside dressing room during IPL match
content: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag was filmed vaping in the team's dressing room during a live broadcast, sparking controversy. The act is illegal in India under the Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act, 2019, raising concerns about athlete health and regulatory compliance. This incident follows a previous breach of protocol by the RR manager.

title: The Phase 2 trust vote in West Bengal: Who will win the Matua votes?
content: Electoral analysts estimate that Matua voters influence outcomes in 60 to 65 assembly seats. In the second phase alone, they are a key factor in 21 constituencies. In tightly contested elections, this scale of influence gives the community leverage that political parties cannot ignore. This is evident in recent electoral trends.

title: ‘Merci, Dhanyavaad…’: Google CEO Sundar Pichai sends message to 1 billion users
content: Google Translate marks two decades, evolving from a niche project to a global AI powerhouse used by over a billion people.  Initially supporting few languages, it now boasts nearly 250, facilitating understanding across borders.  The tool has advanced from statistical methods to neural networks and now utilizes Gemini models for more fluid, real-time translations, even preserving tone and cadence.

title: IPL 2026: For batters, struggle is real in slightly tough conditions
content: Having Been Spoon-Fed T20 Belters, They Have Been Unable To Adapt When Needed Most. Should Indian Cricket Be Worried About Success Away From Home?

title: TMC worker attacked, BJP booth vandalised; Bengal votes amid incidents of violence in Phase 2
content: West Bengal's second phase of Assembly elections commenced amidst reports of violence, voter intimidation, and technical issues. Incidents were reported across Chapra, Shantipur, Nimtala, and Bhangar. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee alleged bias from observers and TMC party flags being removed, while the BJP reported an assault on a polling agent in Chapra.

title: Meghalaya 'honeymoon murder' case: Raja Raghuvanshi’s family shocked after Sonam gets bail
content: The family of slain Indore businessman Raja Raghuvanshi is demanding a CBI probe after his wife, a prime accused in his murder, was granted bail by a Meghalaya court. Raja's mutilated body was found in June last year, and his wife, along with others, was arrested.

title: A day after OpenAI made changes to its relationship with Microsoft, Amazon moves in
content: Amazon Web Services (AWS) swiftly launched OpenAI models on its Bedrock platform, just a day after OpenAI ended its exclusive cloud deal with Microsoft. This move addresses years of customer demand, allowing businesses to access advanced AI models within their existing AWS infrastructure.

title: Watch: 6 wickets in 6 balls! Cricketer scripts history with bowling
content: Myles Davis made cricket history by taking six wickets in six balls for Penkridge Cricket Club against Pelsall Cricket Club in the South Staffordshire County League. Davis finished with figures of 7-16 and described the achievement as “surreal” and “amazing”, with the rare double hat-trick believed to be only the seventh such feat worldwide.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office day 41: Ranveer Singh starrer sails past Rs 1780cr worldwide
content: As theaters continue to buzz with excitement, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' celebrates a triumphant 40-day run, inching ever closer to matching the legendary 'Baahubali 2's box office achievements. This pulse-pounding espionage thriller has raked in an incredible Rs 1780 crore worldwide, bolstered by robust domestic earnings of Rs 1132.99 crore net. Read the full article to know more in detail.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to 'Trump accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: IPL 2026: Will Rohit Sharma play vs SRH tonight? Big update on MI star
content: Rohit Sharma's availability for Mumbai Indians' crucial match against Sunrisers Hyderabad remains uncertain due to a hamstring injury. Despite batting in the nets, the team management is hesitant to risk him unless fully fit. MI's batting struggles, particularly in the Powerplay, have been amplified by his absence, while SRH boasts a formidable batting lineup and an improving bowling attack.

title: Expecting a summer baby? Ideal colour to keep your baby calm, comfortable in nursery
content: Parents can create a serene summer nursery with a specific paint shade - a soft colour that works with natural light to keep babies calm and comfortable. Complementary shades can be used for accents but the main goal is a bright yet soothing environment for your baby's early development at home.

title: 'Politics has its place': Akhilesh Yadav visits BJP MLA who was injured while burning his effigy
content: SP leader Akhilesh Yadav visited BJP MLA Anupama Jaiswal in a Lucknow hospital after she sustained severe facial burns while attempting to set fire to an effigy. Yadav expressed his wishes for her speedy recovery, emphasizing that political differences should not overshadow compassion and human relationships. Jaiswal's husband lauded the visit as an act of "human sensitivity and democratic courtesy."

title: ‘Fair and Lovely babua’: Mahua Moitra takes swipe at ‘Singham’ IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma
content: Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra shared nightclub videos of an Election Commission observer, a UP-cadre IPS officer, amid a controversy over his alleged intimidation of TMC workers. The officer, deployed for West Bengal polls, had previously warned "troublemakers" of "appropriate treatment."

title: Bengal turns fortress for phase-II poll: 2.3 lakh CAPF and 38,297 state cops in place
content: West Bengal's final assembly polls are underway with an unprecedented security deployment across 142 constituencies. Over 2.3 lakh central forces and state police are on duty, alongside observers and NIA teams, to ensure a fear-free and fair election. A significant crackdown has led to over 800 arrests and preventive detentions, targeting potential troublemakers in sensitive zones.

title: Suicide or foul play? Mystery shrouds Bengaluru techie’s death; body found in locked flat
content: A 34-year-old software engineer, Puja Dutta, was found dead in her Bengaluru home under suspicious circumstances. Police suspect suicide due to the door being locked from the inside and cut marks on her body, though investigations are ongoing. Her parents have been contacted and are en route to the city.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today
content: Stock market recommendations: Jio Financial Services Ltd, Hindustan Zinc, and PVR INOX are the top stock recommendations by Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares as the top stock recommendations for April 29, 2026:

title: ‘Lone wolf’ stabbing near Mumbai: Guard’s desperate cries caught on camera
content: A man, identified as Zaib Zubair Ansari, allegedly attacked two security guards at a Mumbai construction site with a sharp weapon. Investigators are probing the incident as a possible "lone wolf" terror attack, with early findings pointing towards radicalization. Digital devices and notes referencing the Islamic State were recovered from his residence.

title: 'Vote in record numbers': PM Modi appeals for high voter turnout in phase-2 of West Bengal polls
content: Prime Minister Modi urged record voter turnout for West Bengal's Phase-2 elections, especially from women and youth. Voting commenced across 142 constituencies, a crucial phase for the TMC's dominance in southern districts against the BJP's challenge. Key contests include Bhabanipur, with extensive security and webcasting in place for 3.21 crore voters.

title: 'Felt defeated so many times': Manu revisits journey amid Sooryavanshi row
content: Two-time Olympic medallist Manu Bhaker celebrated 10 years in shooting with an emotional Instagram post reflecting on her journey, setbacks and achievements. Bhaker recalled overcoming disappointments, including Asian Games near-misses, and revisited her 2018 Commonwealth Games gold. Her post came amid online debate after she was asked about teenage cricketer Vaibhav Sooryavanshi at an NRAI event.

title: 'He’s so cute': Donald Trump says his mother had a 'crush' on King Charles
content: President Donald Trump shared a light-hearted anecdote about his mother's admiration for the British royal family. He mentioned her fondness for Queen Elizabeth II and playfully suggested she had a crush on the young Prince Charles. The comments were made during an official ceremony at the White House to welcome King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the United States.

title: Another day, another record! Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scripts IPL history
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieved a historic feat, becoming the fastest to 400 runs in an IPL season, needing just 167 balls. His explosive 43 off 16 deliveries set the stage for Rajasthan Royals' record chase of 223 against Punjab Kings.  This remarkable performance cements his status as a rising star.

title: West Bengal exit polls 2026: All eyes on Mamata vs BJP — date, timing, when and where to watch
content: West Bengal's crucial assembly elections are nearing their conclusion, with exit polls set to release on April 29 and final results on May 4.  The state witnessed record voter turnout in Phase 1, and Phase 2, covering key strongholds including Mamata Banerjee's Bhabanipur constituency, is now underway.  Prominent leaders from TMC and BJP are in contention.

title: Govt revives strategic sale of IDBI Bank after scrapping bids
content: The government has revived the strategic sale of its stake in IDBI Bank, along with LIC's holding, after previous bids were rejected. A fresh valuation is underway, with officials considering whether to re-engage existing bidders or restart the process to expedite the sale and maintain investor confidence.

title: ‘One game 260, next 70’: Spinner cheekily sums up DCs chaos in IPL 2026
content: Delhi Capitals experienced a dramatic IPL 2026 swing, scoring 264/2 then being bowled out for 75. Young spinner Rehan Ahmed's viral quip perfectly captured this extreme inconsistency. The team's recent three-match losing streak highlights significant challenges in maintaining performance levels at a critical tournament juncture.

title: Leading run scorers of T20 leagues: Virat leads in IPL, Babar in PSL
content: Virat Kohli has etched his name in IPL history, becoming the first to cross 9,000 runs. This remarkable achievement solidifies his status as the league's highest scorer and a global T20 leader for a single franchise. Kohli's unparalleled consistency and loyalty, having played every season since 2008, underscore his dominance in the sport's shortest format.

title: West Bengal polls: Tribunals restore 1,468 votes, delete six names ahead of second phase
content: Supreme Court-mandated tribunals restored voting rights for 1,468 people in West Bengal before the second phase of polls, bringing the total reinstatements to 1,607. This follows the deletion of 27 lakh voters under the "logical discrepancy" clause, with scrutiny intensifying due to a high restoration-to-deletion ratio.

title: EVM 'Glitch', Violence During Early Hours Of Polling In Bengal Phase 2
content: This is the second and final phase of the Bengal election, and it takes place against the backdrop of the controversial EC-mandated Special Intensive Revision, or SIR, of the state's electoral roll.

title: "Deplorable: India On Hormuz Disruption At United Nations Security Council
content: United Nations, April 29 (IANS) India has condemned the attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, which is vital to its energy and economic security, and called for restoring free navigation.

title: 600 km In 6 Hours, 120 kmph Max Speed: All About 6-Lane Ganga Expressway
content: Built at Rs 36,230 crore,Ganga Expressway is Uttar Pradesh's longest expressway and one of India's biggest greenfield road projects.

title: Indore Man's Family Seeks Justice As Meghalaya Honeymoon Murder Accused Gets Bail
content: Raja Raghuvanshi went missing on May 23 last year during the couple's honeymoon, and his mutilated body was found on June 2 from a deep gorge.

title: Black Plastic, Bedsheets: How 2 Hyderabad Women Tried Smuggling Rs 11 Crore Meth In Train
content: The 11.4 kg of contraband, worth approximately Rs 11.4 crore on the black market, was seized under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985.

title: Akhilesh Yadav Visits BJP MLA In Hospital Who Got Injured Burning His Effigy
content: Anuradha Jaiswal was admitted to the hospital after she was injured while setting fire to an effigy of the SP chief during a demonstration.

title: Bengal Elections Phase 2 Voting LIVE: Mamata Banerjee Slams Central Forces As Violence Erupts During Early Hours Of Polling
content: Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 2 Voting LIVE Updates: The second phase of voting for the intense BJP vs Trinamool battle will begin shortly.

title: Mamata Banerjee Vs Suvendu Adhikari In Focus As Bengal Votes In Phase 2
content: Round-2 of the BJP versus Mamata Banerjee contest has started today and is expected to be more riveting than the last phase of the Bengal assembly election. Up for grabs are 142 seats from south Bengal -- including 11 seats from state capital Kolkata

title: Parth Pawar Flies In Plane Of Company Involved In Crash That Killed Father
content: NCP (SP) MLA Rohit Pawar, the nephew of Ajit Pawar, has been targeting VSR Aviation over the Baramati plane crash.

title: Howrah Sweetmaker Turns Bengal Election Fever Into Culinary Art
content: Elaborate Sandesh moulds featuring the grass and flowers of the TMC, the lotus of the BJP, the open hand symbol of Congress and the hammer and sickle of the Left.

title: Exclusive: Trump Shooter's Ex-Classmate On Disagreement With Him On Politics
content: Regarding his views on politics, Elizabeth Terlinden said that she knew the accused through the Caltech Christian Fellowship. She said that their main disagreement about politics happened only because he thought she "spent too much time" on politics

title: Delhi High Court Judge Recuses Self From Hearing Karti Chidambaram's Plea
content: Seeking to quash the case, Karti Chidambaram has alleged in the high court that there was a "gross delay" in registration of the "illegal" FIR on January 1, 2025.

content: King Charles III gave US President Donald Trump a taste of his own medicine at a White House state dinner on Tuesday when he joked that without the...

content: West Bengal assembly elections 2026: NDTV's power-packed team explored the politics of jhalmuri, fish, infiltration, and women's security

title: Poll Body Transfers Bengal Election Official After 'Singham' Officer Row
content: A poll body official said the Joint BDO, Sourav Hazra, has been moved out of Falta and posted to Purulia with immediate effect.

title: "Won't Let Iran Have Nuclear Weapon, Charles Agrees With Me": Trump
content: President Donald Trump said on Tuesday Britain's King Charles did not want Iran to have a nuclear weapon, introducing the fraught

title: JD Vance Fears Pete Hegseth 'Misleading' Trump On Iran War: Report
content: In closed-door meetings, Vance has reportedly questioned the reality of those claims, asking whether the rosy portrayal of the war being presented to Trump is really the truth.

title: US Mandates 'Enhanced' Security Checks For Immigration Applicants
content: President Donald Trump's administration has mandated what it calls "enhanced" security checks for immigration applicants, according to

title: What you need to know about Karnataka’s Rohith Vemula Bill
content: The revised Bill aims to prevent exclusion and injustice while safeguarding the right to education, and the dignity of students, teaching faculty, and non-teaching staff belonging to scheduled castes and scheduled tribes

title: TMC procured 'artificial fingers' to facilitate false voting in Bhabanipur: Suvendu Adhikari
content: Mr. Adhikari also claimed that as many as 3,810 booth slips distributed by Booth Level Officers (BLOs) in Bhabanipur were returned undelivered

title: On TCS harassment and conversion case
content: Concerns have been raised about the Tata Consultancy Services sexual exploitation and alleged forcible conversions case that involves nine FIRs, related to complaints of alleged sexual exploitation, rape and forcible conversion at the company’s office in Nashik, Maharashtra, between 2022 and 2026

title: West Bengal Assembly polls Phase 2 LIVE: Mamata, Abhishek Banerjee visit polling booths in Bhabanipur
content: This phase will decide the electoral fate of more than half a dozen Ministers of West Bengal Government, along with Chief Minister and party chairperson Mamata Banerjee

title: Pariwarvaadi parties not willing to permit Indian women to reach Assembly, Parliament: PM
content: PM vows he will “leave no stone unturned” to ensure that women’s right to reservation in legislative bodies is implemented

title: Harsh sun leaves Kerala’s traditional fishers in distress
content: Searing heat drives fish to deeper, cooler waters, escalating the cost of fishing. Warming waters lead to a surge in population of jellyfish, which tear nets and cause allergies to fishers. The working hours come down, the catch gets spoiled faster, and sun leaves the fish vendors perennially exhausted

title: Women lead new tourism wave in Kerala’s Maravanthuruth
content: Women from Maravanthuruth have launched Kerala’s first all-woman kayaking club, Harithatheeram, under the Responsible Tourism Mission. Led by 63-year-old N.G. Indira, the club aims to boost local tourism by offering kayaking experiences along the Arivalthodu canal, part of the Water Street project.

title: A grief divided: funeral held on the Kishanganga’s banks prompts calls to reopen LoC crossing points
content: Siblings of the dead Kupwara resident, who have lived in POK since 1989, bid farewell to their brother from across the river; LoC crossing points used by such divided families were shut by the Centre in 2019

title: Odisha acts fast after tribal man carries sister’s remains to bank
content: Asked by the bank to submit proof of his sister’s death to withdraw money from her account, the tribal man walked the streets carrying her skeletal remains on his shoulder, a disturbing image that went viral and sparked widespread outrage

title: Odisha tribal produces skeleton of his sister’s body as proof to claim the money in her account
content: Authorities in the Patna block assured that Mr. Munda would be helped in securing a legal heir certificate and other documents required for claiming the money in the account

title: Gujarat backs development model, says CM Patel amid BJP sweep in local body polls
content: BJP has won over half of the total seats in the municipal corporations of Surendranagar, Jamnagar, Porbandar, Karamsad-Anand, Nadiad, Navsari, Vapi, Surat and Morbi.

title: If he is Singham then we are Pushpa: Trinamool candidate on IPS officer Ajay Pal Sharma issuing warnings
content: A poll panel official said Mr. Sharma visited the area after the Election Commission received reports that Mr. Khan's men were allegedly collecting voter identity cards from people in the constituency and threatening them

title: Maharashtra defers action over Marathi rule, to begin training campaign for taxi, auto drivers
content: Transport Minister said while the mandate to speak in Marathi remains in effect, driving licences will not be revoked solely on the grounds of inadequate language proficiency during this period

title: West Bengal goes to the polls in the second phase of Assembly election
content: The 142 seats going to the polls in the second phase are spread across seven districts of south Bengal, including Kolkata; about 3.21 crore electors will determine the fate of 1,448 candidates in the fray

title: Assembly election 2026 highlights: Tribunal clears 1,468 electors to vote in second phase of polls in West Bengal, says official
content: According to the Election Commission, voters whose names appear in the supplementary roll can check details by entering their EPIC number on its website

title: How Tamil Nadu waited for Assembly election results over the decades
content: For the first time in Tamil Nadu’s history, the Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were deployed in all the 234 Assembly segments in the 2001 Assembly elections, mirroring simultaneous rollouts in Kerala, Puducherry, and West Bengal

title: Union government speaking to Anthropic about concerns over Mythos: top MeitY official
content: The impact of artificial intelligence on cybersecurity has emerged as a “very real threat” and it has come up in “very real terms” with the release of Mythos by Anthropic: MeitY Secretary S. Krishnan

title: Rahul meets Nicobarese community, promises to fight against infra project
content: Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha met with Nicobarese community leaders resisting the Union government’s mega infrastructure project, settler and ex-servicemen families, and paid floral tributes to former PM Indira Gandhi at Indira Point

title: Trinamool accuses Election Commission’s police observer of serious misconduct
content: Trinamool Congress has accused 2011-batch IPS officer of Uttar Pradesh cadre, also a trained dental surgeon, of intimidating and threatening locals in Falta under West Bengal’s Diamond Harbour constituency.

title: 44 illegal telecom hubs shut in 2 years: Centre tells Supreme Court
content: Disclosure made during an Inter-Departmental Committee (IDC) meeting chaired by Special Secretary (Internal Security) of Home Ministry in March.

title: ECI recommendation to restrict candidacy from one constituency or collect bypoll expenses from the candidates yet to be implemented, says Madras High Court
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan say, the recommendation made way back in 2004 is yet to translate into a law

title: Over 5,000 police personnel to be deployed in Tiruvannamalai for Chitra Pournami
content: Highways officials said that the festival has been witnessing a steady rise in the number of pilgrims visiting the Arunachaleswarar temple during the festival

title: Residents gherao toll plaza office on Chennai - Bengaluru Highway in Walajah demanding subway
content: The protest comes in the wake of an accident in which a man from Chennasamudram village, was killed after being hit by a tourist van when he tried to cross the busy stretch

title: VIT B.Tech entrance examinations begin
content: The computer-based test will be held from April 28 to May 3 for admissions to the 2026-27 academic year

title: IPL 2026 RR vs PBKS: Ferreira and Shubham combine to end Punjab Kings’ unbeaten streak
content: The two plunder 77 off 32 balls for the unbroken fifth wicket to help Rajasthan Royals chase down the 223-run target; JaiSurya pair sizzles yet again

title: ‘Just peace’ can’t be found by joining organisations that charge fees: UNGA President defends UN system
content: Ms. Baerbock cautioned against ‘unilateral use of force’ while arguing that member states of the UN can mobilise themselves for an institutional action against a state that is accused of genocide by bringing the matter to the UN Security Council

title: CITU petitions ILO over ‘suppression of workers’ rights by Centre and Uttar Pradesh governments’
content: ‘Government of India and the Uttar Pradesh Government are in violation of fundamental ILO principles’

title: J&K govt. blacklists Reliance General Insurance Company Limited for 2 years
content: According to the government, the insurance company failed to issue health insurance cards, provide the list of empanelled hospital, web-based application platform and also failed to finalize performance parameters and penalty framework forming part of the contractual obligations

title: EC asks NIA to ensure bombs not used to disrupt Bengal polls on April 29
content: The directive comes after recovery of crude bombs in the poll-bound State and reports of a minor blast

title: CBI official, ex-ACP get 3-month jail in 26-year-old assault case
content: The case pertains to a controversial search operation conducted on October 19, 2000, at the house of Indian Revenue Service officer Ashok Kumar Aggarwal

title: Dhayanidhi Alagiri approaches Madras High Court against attachment of properties by ED
content: Dhayanidhi’s father M.K. Alagiri filed an affidavit on his behalf and said his son “suffers from intellectual incapacity, remains partially paralysed, and lacks the medical ability to swear the affidavit”

title: ED provisionally attaches ₹1,113-crore assets in Raheja Developers case
content: The ED is conducting an investigation against Raheja Developers Ltd., its director Navin M. Raheja, and other associated persons

content: the defence ministers' meeting was held in the capital Bishkek on tuesday . he called on member-states to eliminate double standards in the global fight against terror . his warning came before his defence minister Khawaja Asif at the meeting .

content: Raj Shekhar Jha is a journalist for the Times of India with over a decade of experience in reporting on national security, terrorism, crime and prisons.

title: West Bengal elections: Phase-1 vote surge new poll rider in TMC’s comfort zone
content: the second phase of vote to elect who will govern Bengal for the next five years has got one extra unlikely parameter . this has turned the vote in what should have been the Trinamool's zone of comfort into a battle of arithmetic and nerves . both parties trying to crystal-gaze into what an unprecedented voter turnout may mean . a voter-turnout bump has meant a larger vote share for the anti-incumbent .

title: India 5th largest military spender at over $92 billion in 2025: Sipri report
content: india's military spending grew by 11% to $11.9 billion in 2025 . the country increased its military spending by 8.9% to $92.1 billion, report says . china, the world's second largest military spender, increased its spending by 7.4% to $336 billion . this was the 31st consecutive year-on-year increase as Chloe slammed .
